data.narrative:
HyImpulse is a Series B-stage aerospace company founded by Mario Kobald that has raised €74 million to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 3 private companies in Space & Aerospace at the same stage, alongside a broader public comp set of 5 traded aerospace names for sector context.

The analysis applies the peer-median method, the only quantitatively defensible triangulation approach given data constraints. Three same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—yield a median valuation of $400M with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. A public-comp anchoring exercise using Space & Aerospace public multiples (median P/S of 53.2x across RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL) could not be applied because HyImpulse's revenue is not disclosed. This is typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M, reflects the peer-median result with modest downside and upside adjustments. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($274M–$1.3B), which suggests material differentiation among Series B aerospace companies, and the absence of revenue data that would permit revenue-multiple validation. The analysis is therefore reliant on stage and sector positioning alone.
